response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is a Staff/Senior Software Engineer with 15+ years of professional experience across a wide range of languages and domains — far beyond the entry-level scope of this role. While the candidate does list Java among their skills and demonstrates strong OOP and agile foundations, the position explicitly requires strong German communication skills, which the candidate rates as only 'Basic' — a critical disqualifier for a client-facing role in the German insurance sector. Placing a Staff-level engineer in a Junior IC position also presents a near-certain retention risk and salary expectation mismatch.
strengths: Proven proficiency in Java and multiple OOP languages (TypeScript, C++, DLang), satisfying the core technical requirement
Strong hands-on agile background including TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and pair programming
CS degree from University of Bucharest and Berlin-based residency, meeting the educational and location requirements
concerns: Massively overqualified — a Staff Software Engineer with 15+ years of experience applying for an entry-level Junior role
German proficiency is self-rated 'Basic', while the role requires strong verbal and written German for client collaboration in the insurance sector
No discernible experience in the insurance domain; background spans ad-tech, fintech, GIS, and embedded systems
